Registry / Terms / type approval

type approval

VIMLOutdated — VIML 2000 G 18 #00192 · 18 instances

Authoritative definition

VIML 2000 · concept #2.6view ↗

decision of legal relevance, based on the evaluation report, that the type of a measuring instrument complies with the relevant statutory requirements and is suitable for use in the regulated area in such a way that it is expected to provide reliable measurement results over a defined period of time

Outdated: Cites VIML 2000. Latest: VIML 2022.
  • VIML 2000 · #2.6
    decision of legal relevance, based on the evaluation report, that the type of a measuring instrument complies with the relevant statutory requirements and is suitable for use in the regulated area in such a way that it is expected to provide reliable measurement results over a defined period of time

Suggested actions

  1. INFO ✓ This term IS in VIML 2022 (concept #2.05). View VIML 2022 entry
  2. MEDIUM 15 distinct definitions — harmonise.

Publication instances

15 distinct definitions across 18 publications. 3 definitions are shared by multiple publications (merge candidates).

2 identical2010202X
decision of legal relevance, based on the evaluation report, that the type of measuring instrument complies with the respective statutory requirements and is suitable for use in the regulated area in such a way that it is expected to provide reliable measurement results over a defined period of time [VIML, 2.6]

2 identical202X
decision of legal relevance, based on the review of type evaluation report, that the type of e measuring instrument complies with the relevant statutory requirements and results in the issuance of the type approval certificate [VIML, 2.05]
Ed.YearPublicationClauseG 18 #Consistency
202X2022OIML D005:20223.2603591pending
202X2021OIML R060-1:20213.1.1103590pending
2 identical2010202X
process of making a decision by a responsible body, based on a review of a type evaluation test report for the type of weights or weight set and professional judgment, that the type is in conformity with the mandatory requirements of this Recommendation for legal applications
Ed.YearPublicationClauseG 18 #Consistency
20102004OIML R 111-1:20042.17.201467pending
